sutta: once the exalted 1 was staying among the barghes on crocodile hill at the deer park in base akala grove now at that time the venable anuruddha was dwelling among the cheitis in the eastern bamboo forest and there these reflections occurred to him as he dwelt alone and secluded this dhamma is for 1 who wants little this dharma is not for 1 who wants much this gamma is for the contented not for the discontented this gamma is for the aloof not for 1 fond of society this dhamma is for the energetic not for the lazy this dharma is for 1 who has set up mindfulness not for the laggard in mindfulness this dhamma is for the concentrated not for the unconcentrated this dhamma is for the wise not for the unwise now the exalted 1 knowing these reflections of the venerable anuruddha as easily as a strong man might stretch forth and bend back his arm and disappeared from the deer pang or bay sakala grove and appeared before him and the exalted 1 sat down on the seat which was made ready then the verbal anuruda saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated the exalted 1 addressed him thus well done well done anuruddha well have you pondered over the 7 thoughts of a great man that is to say this dhamma is for 1 who wants little not for 1 who wants much for the contented not for the discontented for the aloof not for 1 who is formed of society for the energetic not for the lazy for 1 who has set up mindfulness not for the laggard therein for the concentrated not for the unconcentrated for the wise not for the unwise but do you also ponder over this eighth thought of a great man namely this dhamma is for the composed and for 1 who delights in being composed this dhamma is not for the scatter brained or for him who delights in being scatterbrained when anuruddha you ponder over these 8 thoughts of a great man you may and you will enter and abide in the first jhana or in the second jana or in the third jana or in the fourth jana when anuruddha you ponder over these 8 thoughts of a great man and obtain easily at will and without trouble these 4 janas of the higher mind bringing comfort both here and now then will your dust heap rope seem to you as seems a closed chest full of many colored many colored garments to some householder or to some householder's son a thing designed to satisfy for joy for health for well-being for faring to the cool when you ponder thus then will your scraps of arms food seemed to yield as seems a mess of rice cleaned of black grains served with curries and condiments to some householder or his son a thing designed to satisfy then were your lodging at the root of some tree seem to you as seems a gabled house all plastered over with doors bark and shutters closed brought free to a householder a thing to design to satisfy then will your bed and seat be strong with grass seem to you as seems a divan or diamond with a fleecy cover woolen cloth or coverlet sprayed with rocks of dear skins with awnings over it with crimson cushions at either end to a householder a thing designed to satisfy and when anuruddha you ponder over these 8 thoughts of a great man and attain easily at will and without trouble to these 4 genres of the higher mind bringing comfort both here and now then roll your medicine of strong smelling urine seeing to you as seems the varied medicines butter fresh and clarified oil honey and the juice of sugar to some householder a thing designed to satisfy for joy for health or well-being for faring to the cool therefore anuruda dwell here in this eastern bamboo forest among the cheitis during the coming rainy season and the vulnerable anuruddha replied yes lord to the exalted 1 then the exalted 1 when he had charged the vulnerable 1 with this council as a strong man my band stretched forth his arm banished from the chaitis eastern bamboo forest and appeared in the deer town of daysakala grove on crocodile hill among the buggies now the exalted 1 sat down on the seat which was made ready and spoke to the monks thus monks i will make known to you the great the 8 thoughts of a great man listen give heed i will speak yes lord they replied and the exalted 1 said monks what are these 8 this dhamma is for 1 who wants little not for 1 who wants much for the contented not for the discontented for the aloof not for 1 who is foreign of society for the energetic not for the lazy for 1 who has set up mindfulness not for the unmindful for the concentrated not for the unconcentrated for the wise not for the unwise for the compost and for 1 who delights in being composed not for the scatter brain or for 1 who delights therein monks this dhamma is for 1 who wants little this dharma is not for 1 who wants much thus it is said but why is this said here in monks among wanting little does not wish may they know me as wanting little or being contented or being given or given to being aloof or energetic or mindful or concentrated or wise or delighting and being composed does not wish may they know me as such monks this dhamma is for 1 who wants little this dharma is not for 1 who wants much so what is said is said on that account monks this dharma is for the con for the contented this dharma is not for the discontented thus it is said but why is this said here in nuns a monk is contented with each requisite rope arms lodging the medicines for illness so what is said is said on that account monks this month is for the aloof this monk this dhamma this dharma is for the aloof this dharma is not for 1 who is fond of society thus it is said but why is this said here in monks while among dwells in seclusion there come visitors namely monks and nuns lay brothers and lay sisters rajas and the chief ministers call setters and their disciples then the monk with heart inclined towards seclusion leaning towards seclusion bending towards seclusion abiding in seclusion and delighting and renunciation entirely confines his talk to the subject of going apart so what he said he said on that account monks this dhamma is for the energetic this dharma is not for the lazy thus it is said but why is this said here in monks a mom abides stirred to energy to be rid of unwholesome states and to take to wholesome states firm and steadfast he lays not aside the burden of wholesomeness so what he said is said on that account monks this dharma is for 1 who sets up mindfulness this dharma is not for the unmindful thus it is said but why is this sad here in monks among is mindful he is endowed in the highest degree with intendedness of mind and recollection he recollects and calls to mind both the doings and the sayings of long ago so what he said is it on that account monks this dhamma is for the concentrated this dharma is not for the unconcentrated thus it is said but why is this said here in monks among allu from sen's desires etc enters and abides in the first jhana in the second third fourth jhana so what he said is it on that account monks this dhamma is for the wise this dhamma is not for the unwise thus it is said but why is this it here in monks a man is wise he is wise as to the way of growth and decay with aryan penetration into the way to the complete destruction of dukkha so what he said he said on that account then to continue when the buddha said monks this dhamma is for the composed and for 1 who delights in being composed this dhamma is not for the scatter brained or for him who delights in being scatterbrained thus it is said but why is this said here in monks the heart of among leaps up at the thought of the ending of being scattered becomes calm composed and free this dhamma is for the composed and for 1 who delights in being composed this dhamma is not for the scatter brain or for him who delights therein so what he said is it on that account and the vulnerable anuruddha dwelled among the chaitis in the eastern bamboo forest for the duration of the rainy season there dwelling alone secluded earnest jealous resolute he attained not long after in this world by his own knowledge the realization of that unsurpassed consummation of the holy life for the sake of which sons of plans men rightly go forth from their homes to the homeless life he realized birth is destroyed lived is the holy life done is what had to be done there is no more of this to come and available anuruda was numbered among the arahants as the end of the suta
commentary: is a very good suta for a person to practice the the 8 thoughts of a great man you know in the initial part of the suta the buddha said that if a manga practices ponders over these 8 thoughts of a great man and attains the 4 jaanas and all his requisites even though they are simple rope or simple scraps of food and lodging under a tree all these will become to him as being very comfortable and very satisfying because if he attains the 4 jaanas then he has a very blissful abiding in this very life so all these simple things are will be to him becomes as enjoyable as a rich man enjoys all the things of the household life and is mentioned here also that medicine of strong smelling urine you notice um here this is a 1 of the places where it's mentioned but in the vinaya books for the manga it is stated that when a monk takes the ordination he is told by the preceptor that if he were to be sigma then he has to take urine here is stated to be strong smelly strong smelling urine and it is explained in the vinaya books that the monk is supposed to take his urine and keep it for 7 days with medicinal fruits some medicinal fruits you cut them and put them into the urine and keep for 7 days and urine is 1 thing if you if you as soon as it comes out and you drink it now it's not so smelly but if you keep it for a few days it becomes smelly and urine is a very good medicine which works and for most illnesses for most illnesses we take it now it does work well then the later part of the suta talks with the how to practice the a thoughts of a great man a great man is a maha purista the first 1 is the dhamma is for 1 who wants little of you once and it is it now because among his few ones he does not want people to know um that he is a is a he has a good practice he's not 1 who blows his trumpet he is being modest so he does not want people to know his accomplishments that's basically what is meant by a person who by among who have you wants the second 1 he's contented means he's contented with his requisites whatever arms food lodging medicine that he's given he is satisfied now contented and then the practice of aloofness is among dwells usually in seclusion or in a secluded monastery and when lay people or even monastics come to talk to him then he limits his talk so that he can go aside now to practice meditation that is what is meant by being aloof there are 2 types of uh aloofness or seclusion 1 is bodily seclusion in this case they're talking about body base occlusion another 1 is mental seclusion where among practices degrade himself of the developments and and if he rich himself or the development then that is a mental seclusion and then after that this dhamma is for the energetic and he stated here energetic means he um he practices to get rid of unwholesome states and to take to hold some state states for this uh wholesome states and also some states refer to the mental mental states and if you want to get rid of unwholesome states and to attain to hold some states we have to watch our mind all the time the watching the mind is more important than watching the body and the practice of meditation or contemplation is always to watch these 2 things watch the mind and watch the body but the mind is more important because the ego arises in the mind and practicing meditation is not to watch outside things especially the 5 sense doors of seeing hearing smelling taste and touch you're not supposed to watch those as stated in the sutras this dhamma is for 1 who sets up mindfulness not for 1 who is unmindful and here it is stated that he is in doubt in the highest degree with intendedness of mind and recollection he recollects and calls to mind both the doings and sayings of long ago in in the sutas the definition of sati is always the ability to recall what you have done and said a long time ago so this word sati we always know sati as mindfulness but we have to be very careful because that is only a translation the real word sati means the ability to remember and in meditation what we have to remember is to contemplate the nature of body and mind that's 1 aspect contemplate the nature of body and mind not on external things to put your mindfulness on body and mind because that is the source of the self the 5 kandas that is 1 thing the other thing is when you are put when you are contemplating body and mind you have to be with the present stay with the present and not running into the past or into the future by thinking you're not supposed to be thinking when we are practicing contemplation and also the other thing we have to remember when we practice sati is to remember that this body and this mind is not i not mine not myself just try to see that the body and mind is not self basically here and then the next 1 this dharma is for the concentrated not for the unconcentrated and here the buddha defines a person who is concentrated as 1 who attains the 4 channels usually the definition of samadhi concentration is the 4 janus sometimes it is said to be 1 pointedness of mind which means even the first jhana even the first channa but generally the 4 jhanas are necessary because it is stated in the sutasa that when a person attains the fourth jhana then things become very clear to him these things are as they really are and that is at the fourth the state of the fourth channel but some people because they have a lot of wisdom they are very sharp even by attaining the first jhana they can attain and then after that this dhamma is for the wise and the wise is defined as a person who understands the way the nature of arising and seizing of everything in the world everything in the world arises and passes away because of impermanence and so he understands the nature of impermanence and so he understands the nature of dukkha because impermanence brings about dukkha [Music] and this gamma is what the compose not for the scatter brain this i i've used this translation scatter brain the pali word is has to do with papanja which means the proliferation of the mind means a person who thinks a lot uses the mind a lot because you have 1 part and it multiplies that's the meaning of proliferation and to overcome that proliferation of the mind we have to practice samadhi because it is stated in the saka pandha sutra that thinking there is a happiness associated with thinking we like to think generally people like to think and like to imagine that's why we like to see tv i'd like to see shows and all that because there's a story and um and um so that's because of thinking we get that delight and happiness but the buddha said there is a higher happiness and the higher happiness is the happiness that comes from a state of no thinking in the state of no thinking is actually the state of the second jana the state of the second jhana is also called the state of the aryan silence where vitaka and vichara has ceased and europity and sukkah biti and sukha is delight and pleasure which is a higher form of happiness than the happiness you get from thinking and imagining and their dreaming etc so only then can you let go of that happiness of thinking and you don't think so much so these are the ways of practicing the 8 thoughts of a great man and they are very practical advisor the nexus is
